payload.title: The Color Green: Physical and Perceptual Dimensions
payload.content:
# The Color Green: Physical and Perceptual Dimensions

%%CW_CARD_START uid:Green%%
Green, occupying the spectral band roughly 495–570 nm, is the color most associated with nature, growth, and biological vitality. In human vision, green is perceived via medium-wavelength cones (M-cones) and plays a central role in luminance perception. Culturally, green symbolizes safety, environmental consciousness, and in many traditions, renewal. Its complementary color is magenta.
%%CW_CARD_END uid:Green%%

## Physical Basis
- Wavelength range: ~495–570 nm
- Dominant in plant life via chlorophyll
- Used in RGB additive color as one of three primaries

## Perceptual and Cultural Aspects
- High visibility under low light (scotopic vision peaks near 500 nm)
- Universally recognized for 'go' signals and ecological branding
- Contrasts with red in warning/status systems

operations.rationale: Creates a university-level note focused on the color green, embedding the focused card as the central concept.

payload.deleteReason: This note is redundant with mathematics/green-theorem.md and adds little additional value; it contains only a brief restatement of Green's theorem and a reference to the main note.

payload.boundaryBlock:
%%CW_CARD_START uid:Green%%
Green's concept of boundary integrals parallels the architectural principle of defining clear module interfaces — a line integral around a closed curve is analogous to verifying interactions at component boundaries.
%%CW_CARD_END uid:Green%%

operations.rationale: Inserts the Green card into the active note to draw a conceptual link between Green's theorem and architectural coupling.

payload.title: Vault Summary
payload.content:
# Vault Summary

This vault contains notes organized into two main areas: **mathematics** (with a focused note on Green's theorem) and **architecture** (with notes on coupling, reliability patterns, and a copy of the coupling note). A new note on the color green has been added as a topical supplement.

%%CW_CARD_START uid:Green%%
The Green card serves as a conceptual bridge between mathematical integration theorems and architectural interface design.
%%CW_CARD_END uid:Green%%

## Current Structure
- `topics/` — general topical notes (green-color, ...)
- `architecture/` — software architecture notes (coupling, reliability-playbook, coupling - Copy)
- `mathematics/` — math notes (green-theorem)
- `vault-summary.md` — this file

operations.rationale: Creates a summary note describing the vault contents and organization, placed at the vault root for easy reference.

operations.rationale: Confirms the new note is in the intended directory.

payload.boundaryBlock:
%%CW_CARD_START uid:Green%%
Just as Green's theorem connects boundary behavior to interior properties, reliability patterns like circuit breakers connect external failure signals to internal system health.
%%CW_CARD_END uid:Green%%

operations.rationale: Inserts the Green card into a related architecture note to reinforce interdisciplinary connections.
